to: Jeffrey Epstein, Ken Starr, Stephanie Thacker, Guy  Lewis, Martin Weinberg, avid@att.blackberry.net
subject: Re: responsibilities.
body:
We have no communication at all for now with Alex or Jeff or anyone else in that office.   That is critical. 

Sloman has now communicated twice with me so the ball is in my court.  But given the ongoing review, I don't think I want to respond at all yet until I get a sense of how much interaction we will be able to have with Mark's office.  

For now, let's assume that what we want is a package of materials to send to Mark's deputy - and we should decide if we can improve on our big sugal presentation or not.   

We also clearly will want a letter that points out carefully every sloman error in his letter.   At some point, I will probably send that as a response.  

The July 8 deadline is a little further away than would be ideal, but we have the opportunity now to decide if we should enter into a definitive state deal and then arrange to have that ratified in dc as closure.   My instict is that doing so in next few weeks may serve us better than trying to drag out a dag review for several months.  
We can never do better than a state resolution.  So we should aim to achieve that sooner rather than later.  


----- Original Message -----
From: jeffrey epstein [littlestjeff@yahoo.com]
Sent: 06/01/2008 10:06 AM MST
To: Jay Lefkowitz; Ken Starr <Ken.Starr@pepperdine.edu>; Stephanie Thacker <sdthacker@agmtlaw.com>; Guy Lewis <glewis@lewistein.com>; Martin Weinberg <owlmgw@worldnet.att.net>; "avid@att.blackberry.net" <avid@att.blackberry.net>
Cc: me <jeeproject@yahoo.com>; mike tein <tein@lewistein.com>
Subject: responsibilities.



for the moment I've delegated the responsibilities as follows.. 
 
Stephanie -   ceos rebuttal , she  can also speak with authority regarding the paragraphs that reference her involvement
Marty and Mike- , Questions we would like posed , in a perfect world , to the various players .Alex sloman Marie, drew
Darren,-  coordinating a sloman letter rebuttal. includes a line by line analysis of falsehoods,misrepresentations, and inaccurate facts
Jay and team, - structuring an allover presentation..- power point exhibits, transcripts, emails, documents 
Ken Joe, and Alan, deciding the dag hot buttons, 
 question for tomorrows call.. Do we interact with Alex or Sloman in the interim what form.  written only? 
we have to deal with the state deadline. of Jul 8, do we enter a plea with fed consent?if so- to what?
civil cases? new york subpoenas?

body_html:
We have no communication at all for now with Alex or Jeff or anyone else in that office. That is critical.

Sloman has now communicated twice with me so the ball is in my court. But given the ongoing review, I don't think I want to respond at all yet until I get a sense of how much interaction we will be able to have with Mark's office.

For now, let's assume that what we want is a package of materials to send to Mark's deputy - and we should decide if we can improve on our big sugal presentation or not.

We also clearly will want a letter that points out carefully every sloman error in his letter. At some point, I will probably send that as a response.

The July 8 deadline is a little further away than would be ideal, but we have the opportunity now to decide if we should enter into a definitive state deal and then arrange to have that ratified in dc as closure. My instict is that doing so in next few weeks may serve us better than trying to drag out a dag review for several months.
We can never do better than a state resolution. So we should aim to achieve that sooner rather than later.
